hypothesis: In REINVENT4-driven singlet fission optimization, weighting NaviDiv’s fragment penalties by the mutual information between fragment presence and the target score (penalizing only low-MI fragments) will preserve structural diversity (mean pairwise Tanimoto similarity ≤0.12 and unique fragment ratio ≥95% after 1000 steps) while achieving a final mean property score ≥95% of the Baseline (no-constraint) run, outperforming both fixed-threshold fragment-only and combined-constraint regimes.
